channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Why Dan Campbell deserves to win Coach of the Year award over Vikings coach
item.description: Dan Campbell has swept his division, won the NFC North consecutively, and clinched the best record in the conference and the entire league while missing half his roster due to injury.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Carr, Local 4

item.title: Chargers plan to sign Ezekiel Elliott to practice squad after his release by Cowboys
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles Chargers are planning to sign running back Ezekiel Elliott to their practice squad pending a physical, two people familiar with the situation told The Associated Press on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Antetokounmpo needs 3 stitches in finger and returns to post a triple-double in win over Raptors
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Milwaukee Bucks star Giannis Antetokounmpo needed three stitches to close a cut near the base of his right pinkie against Toronto on Monday night, and returned to the game to finish his fourth triple-double of the season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Giants decide to keep Schoen, Daboll
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The New York Giants made a swift decision Monday morning in keeping general manager Joe Schoen and head coach Brian Daboll. Giants fans are upset because they wanted change. Owners John Mara and Steve Tisch thought otherwise. The results are the results. They have been terrible.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Zac Blair tries to regain PGA Tour status
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - The first full field event of the season on the PGA Tour, the Hawaiian Open, tees off on Thursday. Former BYU star Zac Blair flew out to Hawaii to try to Monday qualify because he no longer has full status on the tour.
item.dc:creator: Wesley Ruff, ABC 4

item.title: Utah State cracks the top-25 rankings
item.description: LOGAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - After a 14-1 start, the Utah State basketball team is back in the top-25. The Aggies check in at No. 25 in both the AP Top 25 and USA Today Coaches Poll. Utah State is 4-0 in Mountain West play, under first-year USU head coach Jerrod Calhoun.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: The Sagapolutele saga: How a blue-chip quarterback prospect flipped from Cal to Oregon and back to Cal, all in a few weeks
item.description: Jaron Keawe Sagapolutele signed with Oregon, then transferred to Cal on Sunday to give the Bears their best QB prospect since Jared Goff.
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: Biden signs RFK Stadium land bill into law, a step toward potential Commanders stadium in Washington
item.description: President Joe Biden has signed into law a bill that transfers the land that includes the old RFK Stadium from the federal government to city control.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Whyno, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Castroneves to attempt to qualify for Daytona 500, would be NASCAR debut for 4-time Indy 500 winner
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Four-time Indianapolis 500 winner Helio Castroneves will attempt to make his NASCAR debut in next month's Daytona 500 with Trackhouse Racing.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: David Wright's No. 5 to be retired by New York Mets on July 19
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — David Wright's No. 5 will be retired by the New York Mets before a July 19 game against Cincinnati, the team said Monday. Wright's number will be the 10th retired by the Mets, the sixth since Steve Cohen bought the team ahead of the 2021 season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rays 'expect' city to repair Tropicana Field by Opening Day 2026
item.description: Tampa Bay Rays leaders now say they "support and expect" the City of St. Petersburg to repair hurricane-damaged Tropicana Field by Opening Day of the 2026 season.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Brian Daboll, Joe Schoen will be back with Giants in 2025, team says
item.description: New York Giants co-owner John Mara announced Monday the team will retain both general manager Joe Schoen and head coach Brian Daboll for the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Seahawks' Geno Smith captures big payday despite missing out on playoffs
item.description: Seattle Seahawks veteran quarterback Geno Smith hit a major payday on Sunday despite the team failing to make the playoffs. Seattle defeated the Los Angeles Rams.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aaron Rodgers addresses NFL future following possible final game with Jets
item.description: New York Jets star quarterback Aaron Rodgers showed he could still perform at a high level, but whether he wants to be back playing is still up in the air.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawsuit alleges ex-Fox Sports host Skip Bayless harassed hairstylist, offered her $1.5 million for sex
item.description: The complaint claims Fox executives fostered a hostile work environment that allowed senior managers and on-air personalities including Bayless to abuse workers without fear of punishment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sensabaugh stays hot as Jazz beat Orlando, 105-92
item.description: ORLANDO (ABC4 Sports) - So far, 2025 has treated the Utah Jazz pretty well, especially Brice Sensabaugh.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Cowboys cheerleader drilled in head by kickoff mishap in final game of season
item.description: A Dallas Cowboys cheerleader was drilled by an errant kickoff by Brandon Aubrey during the team's loss to the Washington Commanders in the final game of the 2024 season
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bucs’ Mike Evans makes NFL history, cashes $3 million incentive with catch on final play of game
item.description: Tampa Bay Buccaneers star receiver Mike Evans needed 85 yards to not just make NFL history but cash in a $3 million contract incentive in Week 18.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bears' game-winning field goal drops Packers in NFC playoff seeding, snaps 10-game losing streak
item.description: The Chicago Bears snapped multiple losing streaks as Cairo Santos drilled a 51-yard field goal to drop the Green Bay Packers in the NFC playoff seeding.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mariota leads playoff-bound Commanders to 23-19 win over Cowboys after replacing Daniels
item.description: Marcus Mariota threw a 5-yard touchdown pass to Terry McLaurin with 3 seconds left, lifting Washington to a 23-19 victory over the Dallas Cowboys on Sunday and clinching the sixth seed in the NFC playoffs for the Commanders.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Heat suspend Jimmy Butler for 7 games and will seek to trade him
item.description: Jimmy Butler was Miami's best player for five years, the leader of a team that went to the NBA Finals twice in his tenure. He talked big and, at times, played bigger. He spoke with reverence for the city, for the franchise, for the famed “Heat Culture.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: With chance for 3rd straight win, Bulls fall on road to woeful Wizards, 125-107
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After winning two games in a row to close out 2024, the Bulls seemingly had a great opportunity to kick off 2025 with a third straight victory when they visited the nation's capital on Wednesday night to face the woeful Wizards. Things certainly didn't go as planned for Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Ben Johnson likely to push for a GM besides Ryan Poles, report says
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — Information continues to emerge about the Chicago Bears' looming pursuit of a new head coach. The presumptive top candidate — Ben Johnson — is tied to the latest details suggesting he would want a new general manager if he came to Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: White Sox trade for left-handed reliever from Phillies
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Chicago White Sox traded for a left-handed reliever from the Philadelphia Phillies in exchange for a minor league prospect. In exchange for minor-league right-hander Aaron Combs, the White Sox acquired 31-year-old left-handed pitcher Tyler Gilbert from the Phillies.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Gauff and Fritz put US in United Cup semifinals. Kazakhstan beats Germany to advance
item.description: Coco Gauff and Taylor Fritz powered the U.S., tournament winner two years ago, into the semifinals of the United Cup mixed teams tennis event on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: LeBron James' youngest son Bryce commits to Arizona
item.description: TUCSON, Ariz. (AP) — Bryce James, son of NBA star LeBron James, is heading to Arizona. Bryce James announced his decision Wednesday on Instagram and his father had a quick response. “CONGRATULATIONS MAXIMUS!!,” LeBron James posted.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Illinois holds off South Carolina in Citrus Bowl for first 10-win season since 2001
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — Josh McCray scored his second touchdown of the game on a 9-yard run midway through the fourth quarter to give No. 21 Illinois the lead, and the Illini held on to beat No. 14 South Carolina, 21-17, in the Cheez-It Citrus Bowl on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
